text.title: Frontend Design: Agent Workflow
text.summary: Phase-by-phase agent workflow: requirements capture, brainstorm dispatch, selection and refinement, Storybook and component finalization.

rule.statement: Before dispatching any subagent, collect: surface type (landing | dashboard | form | component set), style direction (minimalist | bold | corporate | playful), tech stack, and brand constraints. Do not brainstorm without these four inputs.
rule.rationale: Brainstorming without a style direction produces the average SaaS aesthetic. Explicit inputs become the diversity constraints that force genuinely distinct variants.

example.code: # Canonical requirements capture requirements = AskUserQuestion([ {"question": "Surface type?", "options": ["landing", "dashboard", "form", "component-set"]}, {"question": "Style direction?", "options": ["minimalist", "bold", "corporate", "playful", "editorial"]}, {"question": "Tech stack?", "options": ["React+TS", "Next.js", "Vue", "HTML/CSS"]}, {"question": "Brand constraints?", "placeholder": "colors, fonts, logo, or 'none'"}, ])

rule.statement: Dispatch `faion-frontend-brainstormer-agent` with explicit diversity constraints derived from requirements. Each variant goes into `designs/variant-N-slug/`. The prompt must forbid shared typeface, palette family, or density across variants.
rule.rationale: Without explicit constraints, LLMs generate one canonical design repeated 3–5 times with minor color shifts. The constraint prohibitions force meaningful divergence.

example.code:
Task( subagent_type="faion-frontend-brainstormer-agent", prompt=f"""Create exactly 3-5 DISTINCT design variants for: {requirements.surface_type} Tech: {requirements.tech}. Style anchor: {requirements.style}.
DIVERSITY CONSTRAINTS (enforce all): - Each variant must use a different typeface pairing - No two variants may share the same color palette family - Density must vary: at least one tight, one airy - Motion: vary from zero to expressive
Each variant must deliver: 1. Working code under designs/variant-{{N}}-{{slug}}/ 2. tokens.css with variant-local tokens (do NOT update globals) 3. README.md with: audience fit, aesthetic levers, trade-offs 4. Runnable as static HTML or Storybook story 5. axe-core pass before submission
Comparison table at the end contrasting all variants on: typeface, palette, density, motion.""" )

rule.statement: Present variants to the user with the comparison table. After selection, dispatch a refinement subagent with only the chosen variant's files — never the full brainstorm context. Re-quote the original spec at every refinement iteration.
rule.rationale: Passing full brainstorm context into refinement exhausts token budget and causes the agent to regress toward rejected variants. Context isolation keeps refinement focused.

rule.statement: After final approval, promote the chosen variant's tokens.css to globals.css or tailwind.config in a dedicated commit. Then dispatch `faion-storybook-agent` for stories and `faion-frontend-component-agent` for typed components. Never merge these into one commit.
rule.rationale: Token promotion is a design decision that deserves its own reviewable commit. Storybook and component generation depend on the promoted tokens; sequencing prevents them from using un-promoted values.

example.code:
# Step 1: promote tokens (human reviews this commit) promote_tokens(f"designs/variant-{N}-{slug}/tokens.css", "src/styles/globals.css")
# Step 2: Storybook Task( subagent_type="faion-storybook-agent", prompt=f"Scaffold Storybook for {project}. Design source: designs/variant-{N}-{slug}/. " f"Create stories for every component variant. Wire Chromatic for visual regression." )
# Step 3: typed components Task( subagent_type="faion-frontend-component-agent", prompt=f"Convert designs/variant-{N}-{slug}/ HTML mocks to typed React components " f"with cva() variants, cn() merging, and Storybook story files." )

antipattern.description: Agent generates 5 variants that share the same Inter font and blue-gray palette.
antipattern.reason: Enforce the diversity constraints explicitly in the prompt. Include a post-generation check: if two variants use the same typeface, regenerate the duplicate before presenting to the user.

antipattern.description: Agent skips a11y audit and delivers variants with contrast failures.
antipattern.reason: Run axe-core per variant during brainstorm, not after selection. Surfacing a variant with WCAG failures wastes selection effort if it must be redesigned.

antipattern.description: Refinement agent receives the full brainstorm context and regresses to rejected variants.
antipattern.reason: Pass only the chosen variant's directory and the user feedback. Never include sibling variant files in refinement context.

antipattern.description: Variants accumulate in designs/ after PR merge and bloat the repo.
antipattern.reason: Add a designs/ cleanup step in the post-merge PR workflow. Archive or delete rejected variant directories after the chosen direction is merged to main.

text.reference: Scaffold a designs/variant-N-slug/ directory with README template.
Full LLM prompt template for generating 3–5 contrastive design variants.
